pass_summaries.summary: Rebuilt Chapter 15 around three concrete collapses: Tomás's exposure and killing in the cafeteria, Marcus's causally grounded late vent disclosure and death, and the stairwell pursuit rendered with full bodily consequence. The revision also cleared the packet's continuity and style-density constraints, including temperature continuity, accented name usage, reduced repetition gloss, and the chapter-level tic counts.

Single finding addressed. The ex-wife relay-message beat at lines 113 and 137 was rewritten to eliminate the 'asked/asks if I'm eating well' cadence that echoed the style-bible calibration example. Both passages now land the same emotional function — mundane domestic concern arriving in a death scenario — through genuinely different phrasing. Marcus's voice (casual, high-contraction, trailing off on personal subjects) is preserved. No collateral changes to surrounding text, chapter structure, heading contract, or continuity obligations.
Compressed the chapter's explanatory aftermath so its must-land beats now resolve through concrete consequence and image rather than repeated thesis language.
